page: Microsoft Azure M64dms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and features an Intel processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs. The server is configured with 1792.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 28.0 GB of memory per core. This hardware profile is designed to support memory-intensive workloads that require massive datasets to reside entirely in system memory.

For storage, the M64dms_v2 includes 2097 GB of local disk storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M64dms_v2 represents a high-resource-density tier where the cost is aligned with the massive memory allocation and dedicated CPU resources. This makes it suitable for large-scale enterprise databases, in-memory analytics, and heavy transactional workloads where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck.
description: Microsoft Azure M64dms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor, it provides 64 dedicated vCPUs and a massive 1792.0 GB of RAM, translating to 28.0 GB of memory per core. The instance features 2097 GB of local disk storage and supports Premium Storage for high-performance disk I/O. Virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, this server does not include dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its high memory-to-CPU ratio and dedicated resource allocation, the M64dms_v2 is engineered for large-scale in-memory databases, transactional systems, and complex data analytics workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint.
